question: A Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot client still sets `zeebe.client.security.plaintext=true`. What does the migration table specify as its replacement?

options.text: There is no replacement property — plaintext vs TLS is now determined by the URL protocol (HTTP vs HTTPS) of the configured client address.
`camunda.client.tls.enabled=false` — TLS is opt-out on the new namespace.
`camunda.client.plaintext=true` — the security prefix is dropped but the flag is kept.

a.text: The migration table leaves the replacement column empty for `zeebe.client.security.plaintext` and explicitly hints: 'plaintext is now determined by the URL protocol (HTTP or HTTPS)'. So you encode it in the address scheme, not in a flag.

b.text: There is no `camunda.client.security.plaintext` property in 8.8; the whole `.security.plaintext` flag is gone, not just renamed.

c.text: `camunda.client.tls.enabled` is not the property the page documents as the replacement; the docs say the URL scheme alone decides plaintext.

d.text: There is no `camunda.client.plaintext` property either; the flag is removed in favour of using HTTP/HTTPS in the client URL.

explanation: In the deprecated `zeebe.client.security.*` block, `cert-path` and `override-authority` get straight renames under `camunda.client.*`, but `plaintext` has no replacement entry. The docs say the new client URL scheme — HTTP for plaintext, HTTPS for TLS — is what now controls transport security.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
